resources.title: CAIE 9701 Stoichiometry · equation coefficients
resources.excerpt: At particle level, balanced-equation coefficients specify the relative counts, or particle ratio, of reacting molecules or formula units in the reaction pattern. A mole is a fixed number of particles: Avogadro's constant, N_A. Scaling every coefficient count by the same N_A preserves the ratio, so the microscopic particle ratio is also the macroscopic mole ratio. Balancing separately ensures the same number of each type of atom appears on both sides; atom accounting is not the cause of the particle-to-mole scaling.

resources.title: CAIE 9701 Stoichiometry · limiting reagent
resources.excerpt: Compare available amounts using the balanced equation before identifying the reagent that limits product formation.

resources.title: CAIE 9701 Analysis · titration evidence
resources.excerpt: A titration calculation needs the balanced reaction, measured volume and concentration evidence before a bounded numerical conclusion is supported.
